usage_notes: Can also refer to a style of cooking (die italienische Küche = Italian cuisine)
common_collocations: in der Küche kochen, eine große Küche haben
image_search_query: modern kitchen with countertops and appliances
image_context: A bright modern kitchen with wooden cabinets and a stove
example_1: Ich koche jeden Tag in der Küche.
example_1_translation: Eu cozinho na cozinha todos os dias.
example_2: Die Küche ist sehr groß und hell.
example_2_translation: A cozinha é muito grande e clara.
example_3: Kannst du mir in der Küche helfen?
example_3_translation: Você pode me ajudar na cozinha?
pronunciation_tips: The 'ü' is a front rounded vowel; 'ch' after front vowels is a soft /ç/ sound
memory_hook: KÜCHE sounds like 'cookie' — where cookies are baked
related_words: der Herd, der Kühlschrank, kochen, das Essen
common_mistakes: Confusing 'ch' pronunciation with /k/ or /x/

usage_notes: Compound word: wohnen (to live/reside) + Zimmer (room)
common_collocations: im Wohnzimmer sitzen, das Wohnzimmer einrichten
image_context: A comfortable living room with a sofa and a television
example_1: Wir sitzen abends im Wohnzimmer.
example_1_translation: Nós ficamos sentados na sala de estar à noite.
example_2: Das Wohnzimmer ist sehr gemütlich.
example_2_translation: A sala de estar é muito aconchegante.
example_3: Im Wohnzimmer steht ein großes Sofa.
example_3_translation: Na sala de estar há um sofá grande.
memory_hook: WOHN = dwell/live + ZIMMER = room → the room where you live
related_words: das Sofa, der Fernseher, sitzen, das Zimmer
common_mistakes: Pronouncing 'w' as English /w/ instead of /v/

usage_notes: Compound: schlafen (to sleep) + Zimmer (room)
common_collocations: im Schlafzimmer schlafen, das Schlafzimmer aufräumen
image_context: A tidy bedroom with a double bed and a wardrobe
example_1: Mein Schlafzimmer ist klein aber schön.
example_1_translation: Meu quarto é pequeno mas bonito.
example_2: Sie schläft jeden Abend früh im Schlafzimmer.
example_2_translation: Ela dorme cedo no quarto toda noite.
example_3: Das Schlafzimmer hat ein großes Fenster.
example_3_translation: O quarto tem uma janela grande.
memory_hook: SCHLAF = sleep → the room where you SCHLAF
related_words: das Bett, schlafen, der Kleiderschrank, das Kissen
common_mistakes: Forgetting that plural of compound nouns follows the last element (Zimmer stays Zimmer)

usage_notes: Compound: Baden (bathing) + Zimmer (room); 'das Bad' is the shorter informal form
common_collocations: ins Badezimmer gehen, das Badezimmer putzen
image_context: A white bathroom with a sink, mirror, and shower
example_1: Das Badezimmer ist neben dem Schlafzimmer.
example_1_translation: O banheiro fica ao lado do quarto.
example_2: Ich putze meine Zähne im Badezimmer.
example_2_translation: Eu escovo meus dentes no banheiro.
example_3: Das Badezimmer hat eine Dusche und eine Badewanne.
example_3_translation: O banheiro tem um chuveiro e uma banheira.
memory_hook: BAD = bath → BADEZIMMER = bath room
related_words: die Dusche, die Badewanne, das Waschbecken, das Handtuch
common_mistakes: Using 'Toilette' as a synonym — die Toilette is specifically the toilet fixture/WC room

usage_notes: Interchangeable with 'die Couch' in everyday speech
common_collocations: auf dem Sofa sitzen, das Sofa ist bequem
image_context: A large comfortable sofa in a bright living room
example_1: Ich sitze gern auf dem Sofa.
example_1_translation: Eu gosto de sentar no sofá.
example_2: Das Sofa ist sehr weich und bequem.
example_2_translation: O sofá é muito macio e confortável.
example_3: Wir haben ein neues Sofa gekauft.
example_3_translation: Nós compramos um sofá novo.
pronunciation_tips: 'S' before a vowel at word start is /z/ in German
memory_hook: SOFA is the same word in Portuguese/German — easy cognate
related_words: der Sessel, das Wohnzimmer, sitzen, bequem
common_mistakes: Using masculine 'der' instead of neuter 'das'

usage_notes: Very common; 'Tisch decken' means to set the table
common_collocations: den Tisch decken, am Tisch sitzen, auf den Tisch legen
image_context: A wooden dining table with chairs in a kitchen
example_1: Das Essen steht auf dem Tisch.
example_1_translation: A comida está sobre a mesa.
example_2: Wir essen zusammen am Tisch.
example_2_translation: Nós comemos juntos à mesa.
example_3: Kannst du den Tisch bitte decken?
example_3_translation: Você pode arrumar a mesa, por favor?
memory_hook: TISCH rhymes with 'dish' — where you put dishes
related_words: der Stuhl, das Essen, decken, die Möbel
common_mistakes: Confusing 'Tisch' (table) with 'Tasche' (bag)

usage_notes: 'Ins Bett gehen' = to go to bed; 'das Bett machen' = to make the bed
common_collocations: ins Bett gehen, das Bett machen, im Bett liegen
image_context: A neatly made bed with white pillows in a bedroom
example_1: Ich gehe um zehn Uhr ins Bett.
example_1_translation: Eu vou para a cama às dez horas.
example_2: Das Bett ist sehr weich.
example_2_translation: A cama é muito macia.
example_3: Sie macht jeden Morgen das Bett.
example_3_translation: Ela arruma a cama toda manhã.
memory_hook: BETT looks and sounds like 'bed' — near-identical cognate
related_words: das Kissen, die Decke, schlafen, das Schlafzimmer
common_mistakes: Using feminine 'die' instead of neuter 'das'

usage_notes: Context determines whether it is a wardrobe (Kleiderschrank) or kitchen cabinet (Küchenschrank)
common_collocations: im Schrank hängen, den Schrank aufmachen, ein großer Schrank
image_context: A tall wooden wardrobe in a bedroom with open doors
example_1: Meine Kleider hängen im Schrank.
example_1_translation: Minhas roupas estão no armário.
example_2: Der Schrank ist zu voll.
example_2_translation: O armário está cheio demais.
example_3: Sie räumt den Schrank auf.
example_3_translation: Ela arruma o armário.
memory_hook: SCHRANK — think of a CRANK used to open a big cabinet
related_words: die Kleidung, hängen, das Zimmer, der Kleiderschrank
common_mistakes: Not distinguishing types of Schrank without a compound prefix

usage_notes: Refers to any open shelving unit; 'das Bücherregal' specifies a bookshelf
common_collocations: ins Regal stellen, das Regal ist voll, ein hohes Regal
image_context: A tall wooden bookshelf filled with books and decorations
example_1: Die Bücher stehen im Regal.
example_1_translation: Os livros estão na estante.
example_2: Das Regal hängt an der Wand.
example_2_translation: A prateleira está presa na parede.
example_3: Ich stelle die Tassen ins Regal.
example_3_translation: Eu coloco as xícaras na prateleira.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable: re-GAL; 'g' is hard /ɡ/
memory_hook: A REGAL shelf looks regal — fit for a king
related_words: das Buch, stellen, die Wand, der Schrank
common_mistakes: Pronouncing as stressed on first syllable (RE-gal)

usage_notes: General term for any lamp or light fixture; 'das Licht' is more abstract (the light itself)
common_collocations: die Lampe anmachen, die Lampe ausmachen, eine helle Lampe
image_search_query: floor lamp living room warm light
image_context: A floor lamp with warm light in a living room corner
example_1: Ich mache die Lampe an.
example_1_translation: Eu ligo a lâmpada.
example_2: Die Lampe auf dem Tisch ist kaputt.
example_2_translation: A lâmpada sobre a mesa está quebrada.
example_3: Kannst du die Lampe ausmachen?
example_3_translation: Você pode apagar a lâmpada?
memory_hook: LAMPE = LAMP + e — almost identical to English
related_words: das Licht, anmachen, ausmachen, hell
common_mistakes: Forgetting the feminine article 'die'

usage_notes: 'Das Fenster öffnen/schließen' = to open/close the window
common_collocations: das Fenster öffnen, am Fenster stehen, das Fenster ist offen
image_context: An open window with white curtains and sunlight coming in
example_1: Bitte mach das Fenster auf.
example_1_translation: Por favor, abra a janela.
example_2: Das Fenster ist sehr groß.
example_2_translation: A janela é muito grande.
example_3: Ich schaue aus dem Fenster.
example_3_translation: Eu olho pela janela.
pronunciation_tips: Final 'er' is a weak /ɐ/ vowel, not a rolled r
memory_hook: FENSTER — think of FERNS behind a window
related_words: die Tür, die Wand, offen, schließen
common_mistakes: Plural is identical to singular: das Fenster → die Fenster

usage_notes: 'Die Tür auf/zu machen' = to open/close the door; 'an die Tür klopfen' = to knock on the door
common_collocations: die Tür aufmachen, die Tür schließen, an die Tür klopfen
image_context: A wooden front door of a house with a brass doorknob
example_1: Bitte schließ die Tür.
example_1_translation: Por favor, feche a porta.
example_2: Jemand klopft an die Tür.
example_2_translation: Alguém está batendo na porta.
example_3: Die Tür ist leider abgeschlossen.
example_3_translation: A porta está trancada, infelizmente.
pronunciation_tips: 'ü' is a rounded front vowel /yː/; the ending is a vocalic r /ɐ̯/
memory_hook: TÜR sounds like 'tour' — you take a tour through the door
related_words: das Fenster, öffnen, schließen, klopfen
common_mistakes: Pronouncing 'ü' as plain 'u'

usage_notes: Refers to the full cooker unit (stovetop + oven); 'der Ofen' is specifically an oven
common_collocations: am Herd stehen, den Herd einschalten, auf dem Herd kochen
image_context: A gas stove with four burners in a kitchen
example_1: Das Wasser kocht auf dem Herd.
example_1_translation: A água está fervendo no fogão.
example_2: Sie steht am Herd und kocht Suppe.
example_2_translation: Ela está ao fogão preparando sopa.
example_3: Vergiss nicht, den Herd auszumachen.
example_3_translation: Não se esqueça de desligar o fogão.
pronunciation_tips: 'd' at end of word is devoiced to /t/
memory_hook: HERD = HEARTH — the heart of the kitchen
related_words: die Küche, kochen, der Ofen, die Pfanne
common_mistakes: Confusing 'der Herd' (stove) with 'die Herde' (herd of animals)

usage_notes: Compound: kühlen (to cool) + Schrank (cabinet). Informal: 'der Kühlschrank' shortened to 'der Kühl'
common_collocations: in den Kühlschrank stellen, aus dem Kühlschrank nehmen
image_context: An open refrigerator stocked with vegetables and dairy products
example_1: Die Milch ist im Kühlschrank.
example_1_translation: O leite está na geladeira.
example_2: Stell das Fleisch bitte in den Kühlschrank.
example_2_translation: Por favor, coloque a carne na geladeira.
example_3: Unser Kühlschrank ist fast leer.
example_3_translation: Nossa geladeira está quase vazia.
memory_hook: KÜHL = cool/chill → KÜHLSCHRANK = the cool cabinet
related_words: die Milch, das Essen, kalt, die Küche
common_mistakes: Pronouncing 'ü' as 'u'

usage_notes: Compound: Geschirr (dishes/crockery) + spülen (to rinse/wash)
common_collocations: den Geschirrspüler einräumen, den Geschirrspüler anstellen
image_context: An open dishwasher in a kitchen with clean dishes inside
example_1: Ich räume den Geschirrspüler ein.
example_1_translation: Eu coloco as louças na lava-louças.
example_2: Der Geschirrspüler ist voll.
example_2_translation: A lava-louças está cheia.
example_3: Stell das Geschirr in den Geschirrspüler.
example_3_translation: Coloque as louças na lava-louças.
pronunciation_tips: Long compound word — break into Geschirr + Spüler; 'sp' at word start in compound is /ʃp/
memory_hook: GESCHIRR = dishes + SPÜLER = rinser → dish rinser
related_words: das Geschirr, spülen, die Küche, der Teller
common_mistakes: Avoiding the word due to length — 'Spülmaschine' is the common synonym

usage_notes: Compound: waschen (to wash) + Maschine (machine)
common_collocations: die Waschmaschine anstellen, Wäsche in die Waschmaschine geben
image_context: A front-loading washing machine in a laundry room
example_1: Ich wasche meine Kleidung in der Waschmaschine.
example_1_translation: Eu lavo minha roupa na máquina de lavar.
example_2: Die Waschmaschine ist kaputt.
example_2_translation: A máquina de lavar está quebrada.
example_3: Kannst du die Waschmaschine anstellen?
example_3_translation: Você pode ligar a máquina de lavar?
memory_hook: WASCH = wash + MASCHINE = machine → wash-machine
related_words: die Wäsche, waschen, die Kleidung, sauber
common_mistakes: Pronouncing 'w' as English /w/ instead of /v/

usage_notes: Used for both sleeping pillows and decorative cushions on sofas
common_collocations: ein weiches Kissen, auf dem Kissen schlafen, Kissen auf dem Sofa
image_context: Soft white pillows on a neatly made bed
example_1: Das Kissen ist sehr weich.
example_1_translation: O travesseiro é muito macio.
example_2: Ich lege das Kissen auf das Sofa.
example_2_translation: Eu coloco o almofada no sofá.
example_3: Sie schläft mit zwei Kissen.
example_3_translation: Ela dorme com dois travesseiros.
memory_hook: KISSEN — when you KISS your pillow good night
related_words: das Bett, die Decke, schlafen, das Sofa
common_mistakes: Plural is identical to singular: das Kissen → die Kissen

usage_notes: Context-dependent: 'die Decke' means blanket when in a bedroom context and ceiling when in a room context
common_collocations: unter der Decke liegen, die Decke ist hoch, eine warme Decke
synonyms: die Tischdecke (tablecloth specific), die Zimmerdecke (ceiling specific)
image_context: A warm blanket on a bed in a cozy bedroom
example_1: Ich brauche eine warme Decke.
example_1_translation: Eu preciso de um cobertor quente.
example_2: Die Decke im Zimmer ist sehr hoch.
example_2_translation: O teto do quarto é muito alto.
example_3: Er liegt unter der Decke und schläft.
example_3_translation: Ele está deitado sob o cobertor dormindo.
memory_hook: DECKE = DECK → a ship deck covers the hull just as a blanket covers you
related_words: das Bett, das Kissen, warm, schlafen
common_mistakes: Ambiguity between 'blanket' and 'ceiling' — rely on context

usage_notes: 'Die Wand' is an interior wall; 'die Mauer' is an exterior or freestanding wall
common_collocations: an die Wand hängen, die Wand streichen, an der Wand
image_context: A white interior wall with a framed picture hanging on it
example_1: Das Bild hängt an der Wand.
example_1_translation: O quadro está pendurado na parede.
example_2: Wir streichen die Wände weiß.
example_2_translation: Nós pintamos as paredes de branco.
example_3: Die Wand zwischen den Zimmern ist dünn.
example_3_translation: A parede entre os quartos é fina.
memory_hook: WAND sounds like 'wand' — a wizard waves a wand at a wall
related_words: das Zimmer, das Bild, streichen, die Tür
common_mistakes: Using 'Wand' for an exterior wall — use 'Mauer' instead

usage_notes: Multi-meaning: floor of a room, ground outside, soil in gardening, or attic in some dialects
common_collocations: auf dem Boden liegen, den Boden wischen, ein sauberer Boden
image_context: A clean wooden floor in a bright living room
example_1: Der Boden ist sehr sauber.
example_1_translation: O chão está muito limpo.
example_2: Das Kind sitzt auf dem Boden.
example_2_translation: A criança está sentada no chão.
example_3: Ich muss den Boden wischen.
example_3_translation: Preciso passar um pano no chão.
memory_hook: BODEN = BOTTOM — the bottom of a room
related_words: die Decke, die Wand, wischen, das Zimmer
common_mistakes: Confusing 'der Boden' (floor) with 'der Keller' (basement)

usage_notes: 'Der Kochtopf' is a cooking pot; 'der Blumentopf' is a flowerpot — context distinguishes
common_collocations: im Topf kochen, einen Topf auf den Herd stellen, ein großer Topf
image_context: A large silver pot boiling on a kitchen stove
example_1: Das Wasser kocht im Topf.
example_1_translation: A água está fervendo na panela.
example_2: Stell den Topf auf den Herd.
example_2_translation: Coloque a panela no fogão.
example_3: Ich mache die Suppe in einem großen Topf.
example_3_translation: Eu faço a sopa em uma panela grande.
memory_hook: TOPF — a TOP with liquid inside, like a cooking pot
related_words: der Herd, die Pfanne, kochen, die Suppe
common_mistakes: Omitting the /pf/ affricate — pronouncing it as plain /p/ or /f/

usage_notes: Specifically a flat pan for frying; 'der Topf' is the taller pot
common_collocations: in der Pfanne braten, eine heiße Pfanne, die Pfanne erhitzen
image_context: A non-stick frying pan with eggs being cooked on a stove
example_1: Ich brate das Ei in der Pfanne.
example_1_translation: Eu frito o ovo na frigideira.
example_2: Die Pfanne ist sehr heiß.
example_2_translation: A frigideira está muito quente.
example_3: Kannst du die Pfanne waschen?
example_3_translation: Você pode lavar a frigideira?
pronunciation_tips: Initial 'Pf' is a German affricate /pf/ — both sounds must be pronounced
memory_hook: PFANNE starts with Pf like 'pan' with a German twist
related_words: der Topf, braten, der Herd, das Öl
common_mistakes: Dropping the /p/ in 'Pf' and pronouncing it as /f/ alone

usage_notes: 'Einen Teller spülen' = to wash a plate; 'den Tisch decken' involves Teller
common_collocations: den Teller abwaschen, Essen auf den Teller geben, ein sauberer Teller
image_context: A clean white ceramic plate set on a dining table
example_1: Leg das Essen bitte auf den Teller.
example_1_translation: Por favor, coloque a comida no prato.
example_2: Der Teller ist noch schmutzig.
example_2_translation: O prato ainda está sujo.
example_3: Wir haben vier Teller auf dem Tisch.
example_3_translation: Temos quatro pratos sobre a mesa.
pronunciation_tips: Final 'er' = weak /ɐ/; double 'l' does not change pronunciation
memory_hint: TELLER sounds like 'teller' at a bank — they hand you something flat
memory_hook: TELLER — a bank TELLER slides things across a flat surface, like a plate
related_words: die Tasse, die Gabel, der Löffel, das Messer
common_mistakes: Plural is identical to singular: der Teller → die Teller

usage_notes: Part of the standard cutlery set alongside Messer and Löffel
common_collocations: mit der Gabel essen, Gabel und Messer, eine Gabel nehmen
image_context: A silver fork placed on the left side of a plate
example_1: Ich esse das Fleisch mit der Gabel.
example_1_translation: Eu como a carne com o garfo.
example_2: Die Gabel liegt links vom Teller.
example_2_translation: O garfo fica à esquerda do prato.
example_3: Kannst du mir eine Gabel geben?
example_3_translation: Você pode me dar um garfo?
memory_hook: GABEL has a 'G' — prongs GRAB the food
related_words: das Messer, der Löffel, der Teller, essen
common_mistakes: Forgetting feminine article 'die'

usage_notes: The kitchen/table knife; 'ein scharfes Messer' = a sharp knife
common_collocations: mit dem Messer schneiden, ein scharfes Messer, Gabel und Messer
image_context: A sharp kitchen knife on a wooden cutting board
example_1: Ich schneide das Brot mit dem Messer.
example_1_translation: Eu corto o pão com a faca.
example_2: Das Messer ist sehr scharf.
example_2_translation: A faca está muito afiada.
example_3: Das Messer liegt rechts vom Teller.
example_3_translation: A faca fica à direita do prato.
pronunciation_tips: Short 'e' /ɛ/; double 's' confirms short vowel; final 'er' = /ɐ/
memory_hook: MESSER sounds like 'messer' — a MESS-er cuts through the mess
related_words: die Gabel, der Löffel, schneiden, der Teller
common_mistakes: Using feminine 'die' or masculine 'der' — it is neuter 'das Messer'
